facts.statement: Costco operates membership warehouses and e-commerce sites based on offering low prices on a limited selection of nationally branded and private-label products across a wide range of categories, aiming for high sales volumes and rapid inventory turnover.

Costco says volume purchasing, efficient distribution, reduced handling in no-frills self-service warehouse facilities, and rapid inventory turnover allow it to operate profitably at significantly lower gross margins than most other retailers.

Costco buys most merchandise directly from suppliers and routes it to cross-docking consolidation points, or depots, or directly to warehouses; depots receive large supplier shipments and quickly ship goods to warehouses to create freight-volume and handling efficiencies.
Costco operated 914 warehouses worldwide at August 31, 2025, up from 890 at September 1, 2024 and 861 at September 3, 2023.

Costco's membership warehouses operate in the United States and Puerto Rico, Canada, Mexico, Japan, the United Kingdom, Korea, Australia, Taiwan, China, Spain, France, Sweden, Iceland, and New Zealand.
Costco operates e-commerce sites in the U.S., Canada, Mexico, the U.K., Korea, Taiwan, Japan, and Australia.
Costco carries less than 4,000 active SKUs per warehouse in its core warehouse business and averages from 9,000 to 10,000 SKUs online, some of which are also available in warehouses.

Costco's core merchandise categories are Foods and Sundries, Non-Foods, and Fresh Foods; Warehouse Ancillary includes gasoline, pharmacy, optical, food court, hearing aids, and tire installation; Other Businesses include e-commerce, business centers, travel, and other operations.
Costco operated 747 gas stations at the end of fiscal 2025, and its gasoline business represented approximately 10% of total net sales in 2025.
E-commerce net sales represented approximately 7% of Costco's total net sales in fiscal 2025, while digitally enabled sales represented approximately 10% of total net sales.

At the end of fiscal 2025, Costco had 81.0 million total paid members, including 68.3 million Gold Star members and 12.7 million Business members including affiliates; total cardholders were 145.2 million.
Executive members represented 38.7 million of Costco's paid members in 2025 and accounted for approximately 73.6% of worldwide net sales in 2025.
Costco's member renewal rate at the end of fiscal 2025 was 92.3% in the U.S. and Canada and 89.8% worldwide, excluding affiliates of Business members.
For fiscal 2025, Costco reported total revenue of $275.235 billion, including total net sales of $269.912 billion.

In fiscal 2025, Costco's United States segment generated $200.046 billion of total revenue and $6.878 billion of operating income; Canada generated $36.923 billion of total revenue and $1.849 billion of operating income; Other International generated $38.266 billion of total revenue and $1.656 billion of operating income.

Costco's fiscal 2025 net sales by merchandise category were $109.564 billion for Foods and Sundries, $71.190 billion for Non-Foods, $37.988 billion for Fresh Foods, and $51.170 billion for Warehouse Ancillary and Other Businesses.

Costco reported fiscal 2025 merchandise costs of $239.886 billion, selling, general and administrative expenses of $24.966 billion, operating income of $10.383 billion, income before income taxes of $10.818 billion, and net income of $8.099 billion.
Costco's fiscal 2025 additions to property and equipment totaled $5.498 billion, with $4.215 billion in the United States, $580 million in Canada, and $703 million in Other International.

Costco employed 341,000 employees worldwide at the end of fiscal 2025, including 223,000 in the United States, 55,000 in Canada, and 63,000 in Other International; approximately 95% were employed in membership warehouses and distribution channels, and around 5% were represented by unions.

Costco reported approximately 94% retention in 2025 in the U.S. and Canada for employees with at least one year of service, increased the U.S. and Canada entry-level starting wage by $0.50 per hour to at least $20.00 in March 2025, and reported an average hourly rate of approximately $32.00 for U.S. hourly employees at the end of 2025.

Costco's 2025 Form 10-K was signed by Ron M. Vachris as Chief Executive Officer, President and Director; Gary Millerchip as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer; Hamilton E. James as Chairman of the Board; and Tiffany M. Barbre as Senior Vice President and Corporate Controller.

Costco's proxy states that the Board had ten nominees for election at the 2026 annual meeting, the Compensation Committee is composed of independent directors, and equity compensation in the form of restricted stock units is the largest component of executive compensation if fully earned through achievement of performance targets.

Costco describes its industry as highly competitive and names Walmart, Target, Kroger, and Amazon as significant general merchandise retail competitors in the U.S., as well as Sam's Club and BJ's Wholesale Club as warehouse-club competitors.

Costco disclosed pending matters including California wage-and-hour and PAGA actions, opioid-related litigation, privacy-law suits related to pixel trackers on Costco.com, a baby-wipes PFAS false-advertising case, a False Claims Act investigation concerning prescription medications, and an EPA FIFRA matter that the company said had reached an agreement to settle for an immaterial amount.
